findings.claim: Q1 distractor B is labeled 'reversed_causality' in both generator_meta.planted_traps and the integrated/pedagogy rationale, but the trap is not a causal inversion.
findings.evidence: Rationale: 'B är reversed_causality: den vänder på vad som fastnar och vad som passerar'. Option B swaps which substance is retained vs. passes ('framför allt fånga upp det lösta vägsaltet, medan de grova partiklarna följde med vidare') — an attribute/category swap of the finding, not the inversion of a cause-effect relation. No causal claim exists in P3 to reverse.
findings.why: The label names a real reversal but mis-specifies its type; the option is still unambiguously false and the key (C) is unaffected, so no stage verdict changes.

findings.claim: Q1 distractor D rationale frames the scope_shift as moving an outflow-level drop to the whole lake, but the passage reports no salt drop at the outflow either.
findings.evidence: Rationale: 'flyttar fyndet från utflödet vid bassängen till hela sjön'. The passage's outflow finding is that dissolved salt 'passerade i det närmaste oförändrat rakt igenom fällan' (no drop anywhere). D ('Salthalten i Klarasjön sjönk märkbart') asserts a drop the text explicitly says is unestablished — a contradicted overclaim more than a relocated finding.
findings.why: The essential judgment (D contradicts the text; correctly keyed out) is right; only the mechanism-name is loose. Not verdict-changing.
